# Kanban Pro — Agent Ruleset (Summary)

> Full ruleset: see `README.md` at the project root.

## Critical Rules

1. **Tickets are Markdown files** in `tickets/` with strict YAML frontmatter.
2. **Filename must match the `id` field** and end in `.md`. Use `T-<timestamp>` IDs.
3. **Required frontmatter fields**: `id`, `title`, `status`, `rank`, `created`, `type`, `priority`, `assignee`, `tags`, `modified`, `modifiedBy`.
4. **`status` must be a valid column ID** from `.kanban/board.json`. Invalid values → "Uncategorized".
5. **Atomic writes only** — rewrite the entire file. Partial writes cause corruption.
6. **Read `MAPPING.md` first** for a token-efficient board overview before opening ticket files.
7. **After modifying tickets**, regenerate `MAPPING.md` (see README §10).
8. **Your assigned ticket is your working memory** — persist plans, checklists, and notes in the ticket body, never in `/tmp` or hidden scratchpads (see README §18).

## Team Graph & Agent Runtimes (Summary)

- **`ORGANOGRAM.md` (project root)** lists every member (humans + agents) and which CLI / SDK runtime each one is bound to. Read this to know who is on the team.
- **`.kanban/agents.yaml`** is the agent-runtime registry. Three tiers: Tier-1 recognised CLIs (claude, codex, cursor-agent, aider, amp, opencode, agy, omc, goose), Tier-2 frameworks, Tier-3 custom adapters. See README §14–§15.
- **`kp`** (forthcoming) is the bounded write-surface CLI; until it ships, edit ticket files directly with the conventions above.

For full field definitions, custom fields, sprint/epic conventions, linking rules, Gantt
configuration, the team-graph schema, and per-OS install commands for all 9 canonical Tier-1 CLIs,
read `README.md` in the project root.
